---
title: "Animal Match-Ups"
description: |
  Creatures of the world
date: '2026-05-22'
image: "crowraven.jpg"
categories:
    - Animals
format: html
toc: true
lightbox: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  error: false
  message: false
draft: false
---

```{r}
library(here)
source(here("packages.R"))
source(here("functions.R"))

series_filter = 13

data = pollresults(series_filter)
```

```{r}
simple_bar_question(data, "Spiky")
```

```{r}
simple_bar_question(data, "Pinnipeds")
```

```{r}
simple_bar_question(data, "Insect-Eaters")
```

```{r}
simple_bar_question(data, "Crocodylia")
```

```{r}
simple_bar_question(data, "Corvidae")
```

```{r}
simple_bar_question(data, "Lepidoptera")
```

```{r}
simple_bar_question(data, "Anura")
```

# How decisive were these results, combined?

Seven animal kingdom grudge matches this week.

```{r}
#| label: summary-stats
summary_am = summarize_two_option(data)
lopsided_am = summary_am %>% arrange(p_value_50_50)
closest_am  = summary_am %>% arrange(desc(p_value_50_50)) %>% slice(1)
```

**TL;DR:** Hedgehogs steamrolled Porcupines (`r lopsided_am$leader_votes[1]`–`r lopsided_am$trailer_votes[1]`, `r percent(lopsided_am$leader_share[1],1)`), the most one-sided result of the week. `r closest_am$question[1]` was the nail-biter: `r closest_am$leader[1]` over `r closest_am$trailer[1]` by just `r closest_am$margin_votes[1]` votes (p = `r signif(closest_am$p_value_50_50[1],2)`) — essentially a coin flip between the two reptiles.

```{r}
#| label: summary-table
binom_summary_table(summary_am)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-leader-ci
#| fig-cap: "Leader shares with 95% confidence intervals, ranked from most lopsided to closest."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
summary_am %>%
  mutate(question = fct_reorder(question, leader_share)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = question, x = leader_share, color = question)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_lower, xmax = ci_upper), height = 0.15) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = 2, color = "gray60") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(x = "Leader's share of votes", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

# Does HQ agree with RTP?

```{r}
#| label: hq-rtp-stats
hq_rtp_am = hq_rtp_compare(data)
alignment_am = cor(hq_rtp_am$hq_share, hq_rtp_am$rtp_share)
biggest_gap_am = hq_rtp_am %>% arrange(desc(abs(gap))) %>% slice(1)
```

```{r}
#| label: hq-rtp-table
hq_rtp_am %>%
  transmute(
    question,
    `Leader (overall)` = option,
    `HQ share`  = sprintf("%s (%d/%d)", percent(hq_share, 1), hq_votes, hq_N),
    `RTP share` = sprintf("%s (%d/%d)", percent(rtp_share, 1), rtp_votes, rtp_N),
    `Gap (pts)` = percent(abs(gap), 1),
    `p-value`   = signif(p_value, 3)
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-hq-rtp
#| fig-cap: "HQ vs RTP support for each question's overall winner."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4
hq_rtp_plot(hq_rtp_am)
```

```{r}
#| label: cumulative-alignment
cumulative_am = cumulative_alignment(13)
```

None of the individual gaps clear statistical significance this week (all p > 0.28) — but the week-over-week Alignment Index takes its biggest tumble yet, down to **`r round(alignment_am, 2)`** (cumulative, smoothed over both RTP-era polls so far: **`r round(cumulative_am, 2)`**). No single matchup is a smoking gun; it looks more like everything drifted slightly in the same direction at once.

The widest gap belongs to `r biggest_gap_am$question[1]`, where HQ leaned toward `r biggest_gap_am$option[1]` (`r percent(biggest_gap_am$hq_share[1],1)`) while RTP was almost perfectly torn — RTP's raw vote on that question split exactly down the middle. It's a good reminder that "the office average" can paper over a genuine local difference of opinion, even when it's not big enough to call statistically significant yet.
